ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general pasta food product class (FOODON_00002473); no specific truffle cream pasta entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers cooked pasta as a category. No specific truffle cream pasta code exists; closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 229953004 covers pasta as a food concept; no truffle cream pasta-specific code exists. Closest parent used.

allergens: dairy (standard version; vegan option available dairy-free)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of pasta with truffle cream sauce is approximately 350 g, typically comprising a full plate of cooked pasta dressed with truffle-infused cream sauce.

nutrient_source.title: Pasta, cooked, enriched, without added salt — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 20121); supplemented with cream sauce component estimates from USDA Foundation Foods
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy cooked pasta (NDB 20121) as the carbohydrate and protein base, combined with USDA Foundation Foods heavy cream (FDC ID 170859) to model the truffle cream sauce component. A composite ratio of approximately 65% cooked pasta and 35% cream sauce by weight was applied. Truffle oil contributes negligible macro change at typical usage levels. This is an estimation; exact restaurant formulation is not publicly available. Vegan variant substitutes plant-based cream; macros are similar.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — pasta, white, boiled (GI = 44, reference glucose)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 44 is taken from the University of Sydney GI Database for white wheat pasta, boiled (mean GI referenced to glucose). Truffle cream sauce adds fat and protein which further attenuates glycemic response; the GI of 44 is therefore a conservative upper-bound estimate for this dish. GL per serving calculated as: (44 × 23.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 350 g serving) / 100 = 36.2. gi_category is 'low' per the ≤55 threshold rule.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~210 mg/100 g; ~735 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: A single 350 g restaurant serving provides approximately 735 mg sodium, representing about 32% of the 2,300 mg daily limit recommended by health authorities; individuals with hypertension should request reduced-salt preparation or limit portion size.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (GL ~36 per serving) and refined carbohydrate content
type2_diabetes.guidance: Although pasta has a low GI (44), the large restaurant portion (350 g) delivers a high glycemic load of approximately 36 per serving; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consider a half-portion, choose wholegrain pasta where available, and pair with non-starchy vegetables to moderate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from cream (~5.6 g/100 g; ~19.6 g per serving) and sodium load
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The cream-based sauce contributes approximately 19.6 g of saturated fat per serving, exceeding the typical daily limit of 20 g for cardiovascular risk reduction; individuals with cardiovascular disease should opt for the plant-based vegan variant with reduced cream or request a lighter sauce preparation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pasta with Truffle Cream Sauce is a Western-style pasta dish featuring cooked wheat pasta (typically spaghetti, fettuccine, or tagliatelle) tossed in a rich cream sauce infused with truffle oil or shaved black or white truffle. The dish originates from Italian culinary tradition and has been widely adopted in contemporary global restaurant menus, including in Singapore where it is served at casual-dining and café-style establishments. The sauce is typically prepared by reducing heavy cream with garlic, finishing with truffle oil or truffle paste, and optionally incorporating parmesan cheese. A plant-based vegan variant substitutes dairy cream with oat or cashew cream and omits cheese, making it accessible to vegan diners. The flavour profile is intensely savoury and earthy from the truffle, with a rich, coating mouthfeel from the cream. Nutritionally, the dish is energy-dense (approximately 198 kcal per 100 g) with moderate protein (5.2 g/100 g), significant fat from cream (9.8 g/100 g, of which 5.6 g is saturated), and a moderate carbohydrate load from pasta (23.5 g/100 g). The glycemic index of pasta is low (GI ~44), but the large restaurant portion size results in a high glycemic load per serving (~36), which is clinically relevant for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome. Sodium content is moderate to high at restaurant preparation levels (~210 mg/100 g), warranting attention for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The dish is vegetarian in its standard form and vegan when prepared with plant-based cream.
common_user_queries: How many calories in truffle pasta?
Is truffle pasta suitable for diabetics?
Can I eat truffle cream pasta if I have high blood pressure?
Is there a vegan truffle pasta option?
What is the GI of pasta with cream sauce?
How much sodium is in truffle cream pasta?
Is truffle pasta high in saturated fat?
What pasta dishes are available at Flavours by Saute Funan?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy cooked pasta and USDA Foundation Foods heavy cream using a composite ratio model, as no exact nutritional analysis for this specific restaurant dish is available in any approved primary source. GI is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for analogous white pasta. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are high-quality approved databases and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (cooked pasta, NDB 20121) and USDA Foundation Foods (heavy cream, FDC ID 170859) at an approximate 65:35 pasta-to-sauce ratio by weight. Exact restaurant formulation from Flavours by Saute - Funan is not publicly available in any approved source. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not meaningfully reported for this food type in the USDA source databases used; cream contributes negligible vitamin D at typical sauce quantities. 3. The vegan plant-based variant (available at no extra charge per the source description) would have lower saturated fat and zero cholesterol; the values here represent the standard cream-based version. 4. GI of 44 is a conservative analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for white wheat pasta boiled; the cream sauce fat content would further lower the effective GI in practice. 5. GL per serving = (44 × 23.5 × 350) / 10000 = 36.2. 6. Allergen note: standard version contains gluten and dairy; vegan variant is dairy-free but still contains gluten. 7. Halal status is not confirmed from the source listing; consumers requiring halal certification should verify directly with the restaurant. 8. The cuisine is classified as Global/Western per the input source tags and the dish's Italian origin adapted for the Singapore market. 9. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean market context (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as this dish is served in Singapore. 10. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA component sources and are approximate. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source databases for this food type.

Iteration 2: Verified GI category threshold (GI=44 ≤ 55 → 'low' confirmed). Verified ontology match_type values are all within allowed set (exact/closest_parent/none). Verified no zero core macros requiring justification. Confirmed GL calculation: (44 × 23.5 × 350)/10000 = 36.19 ≈ 36.2. All hard gates and Q1–Q8 pass. Final decision: PASS.

Iteration 3: Addressed validator feedback for Q8. Identified a subtle spelling difference in the Hindi alias 'ट्रफल क्रீम सॉस पास्ता' (long 'ee') in `aliases_by_language` versus 'ट्रफल क्रीम सॉस पास्ता' (short 'i') in `aliases`. Corrected `aliases_by_language.hindi` to use 'ट्रफल क्रीम सॉस पास्ता' to ensure perfect consistency with the flat `aliases` list, resolving the 'aliases_by_language entries missing from flat aliases' error. Updated iteration count and timestamp.
